subparts.text: Explain the role of reclosers and sectionalizers in radial distribution system protection. 5
Describe the function of SCR-based static switches in source switching. 5
Write short notes on the use of Per-Unit analysis in fault analysis of distribution systems. 5
Define flicker and mention its effect on power quality in DG systems. 5
Briefly explain thermal impedance and its role in damage estimation due to thermal cycles. 5
Differentiate between passive and active methods for detecting unintentional islanding in distribution networks. 5

questions.question_text: Briefly explain the different distributed generation technologies. Why they are called distributed generation?
Perform a sequence component analysis for a faulted distribution system and explain its implications on DG operation.
Discuss the impact of DG integration on loop and secondary network distribution grids.
How the upstream and downstream circuit breakers are coordinated when faults occur at different zones?
Briefly explain passive and active islanding detection techniques by explaining at least one example of each.
Describe the inverter control strategies for both grid-connected and stand-alone DG operations. Include protection schemes.
Explain the power quality implications of DG with reference to flicker, reactive power compensation, and low voltage ride through requirements.
Evaluate the different topologies of power converters used in DG applications with their merits and limitations
